title: The Past, Present, and Future of Superhero Movies | The Big Picture (Ep. 35)
format: Sean, Jason Concepcion, and David Shoemaker trace superhero movies from Blade's surprise hit through the MCU's assembly line, DC's shaky search for its own voice on the eve of Justice League, and what's next once Infinity War hands the franchise off to a new cast.

segments.heading: Blade to the MCU
segments.note: how genre movies built the modern superhero movie

films.title: Blade
films.note: Wesley Snipes' vampire hunter, a surprise hit ($131.2 million on a $45 million budget) that proved a genre movie could carry a C-list Marvel character nobody outside the comic shop had heard of.

films.title: X-Men
films.note: Bryan Singer's launch of the modern era, and the template problem that came with it: introduce a dozen characters at once and hope the act-three fight earns the emotional weight.

films.title: Watchmen
films.note: Zack Snyder's faithful adaptation of the Alan Moore/Dave Gibbons graphic novel. An easier job, the hosts argue, because there was a hard stop to the source material.

films.title: Batman Begins
films.note: Christopher Nolan's start of the Dark Knight trilogy, five years after X-Men, and the beginning of DC's belated answer to a shared-universe strategy.

films.title: Iron Man
films.note: Robert Downey Jr., a total gamble at the time, and a $300 million run that stunned even Zack Snyder: the film that kicked off the MCU brand.

films.title: Thor
films.note: Chris Hemsworth's introduction, described here as a 'loss leader': the movie mattered less than the stinger scenes tying it to Avengers.

films.title: Captain America: The First Avenger
films.note: Alongside Thor, one of the two films Zack Snyder mocked before either came out: 'Captain America movie? What?'

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
films.note: Marvel's biggest flex: a team nobody had heard of (Rocket Raccoon, a wrestler, Chris Pratt doing sit-ups) that still made $300-plus million on brand alone.

films.title: Ant-Man
films.note: Proof the MCU could coast on infrastructure after Guardians: greenlit because 'let's do Ant-Man,' and now considered one of the more beloved entries.

films.title: Thor: Ragnarok
films.note: 'The Thor movie that came out two weeks ago,' cited as one of the five best Marvel movies, after two lesser films spent years finding the character's tone.

segments.heading: DC's Uneven Rise
segments.note: on the eve of Justice League

films.title: Man of Steel
films.note: Zack Snyder's Superman reboot, commercially successful but criticized for a city-leveling final battle with Zod that never reckons with its civilian toll.

films.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
films.note: Overcorrects for that criticism with newscasters narrating an empty battlefield: a symptom, the hosts argue, of DC not trusting its own storytelling voice.

films.title: Spider-Man
films.note: Invoked in the same breath as Wonder Woman for how to do an origin right: the Green Goblin's story is fused directly into Peter Parker's.

films.title: Hellboy
films.note: Cited alongside Spider-Man as a villain-driven origin done well: 'it's the Nazis.'

films.title: Suicide Squad
films.note: A trailer that sold a Marvel-style movie the finished film wasn't, chasing Deadpool's success through extensive reshoots. Margot Robbie's Harley Quinn is the one thing that landed.

films.title: Wonder Woman
films.note: The first DCEU movie that isn't reacting to something else: Patty Jenkins's singular vision, closer to Marvel's trust-the-director model than anything else DC has made.

films.title: Justice League
films.note: Unreleased at recording; the hosts haven't seen it. Has to sell Cyborg, Aquaman, and the Flash as future tentpoles while patched together by Joss Whedon after Zack Snyder left following a family tragedy.

segments.heading: Batman and the Rogues Gallery
segments.note: the Nolan trilogy and Heath Ledger's Joker

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: Heath Ledger's Joker, cited as the main reason people still talk about the Nolan trilogy in glowing terms; his death left open what future Batman/Joker stories might have looked like.

films.title: The Dark Knight Rises
films.note: Referenced via Jason's imagined Aquaman line to Batman: 'Do you know how many fucking fish died when you exploded that nuclear weapon?'

films.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past
films.note: Loved by the hosts despite bending a classic comic arc to accommodate Jennifer Lawrence's contract: self-defeating world-building in service of a bigger star.

films.title: X-Men: Apocalypse
films.note: Grouped with Days of Future Past as a middling-to-dissatisfying execution of a classic comic storyline.

films.title: X-Men: The Last Stand
films.note: Kills off Cyclops (James Marsden) off-screen in the first half hour: emblematic, the hosts argue, of the franchise underserving its own characters.

films.title: X-Men: First Class
films.note: Jason's exception to the franchise's mediocrity: 'it had a certain style that I liked.'

films.title: Deadpool
films.note: Ryan Reynolds's solo movie reset the model: tell the truest version of one character instead of shoehorning in the whole roster.

films.title: Logan
films.note: The corrective: two characters, a strong supporting cast, and the Old Man Logan influence. Universally loved on this podcast.

films.title: The New Mutants
films.note: Pitched at the time, off a trailer, as a horror movie: X-Men going full genre picture, echoing Blade's original gambit.

films.title: Dark Phoenix
films.note: The next planned X-Men film, retelling the Phoenix saga with Sophie Turner, after The Last Stand's botched version of the same story.

segments.heading: What's Next for Marvel
segments.note: Infinity War and the handoff to a new generation

films.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
films.note: Tom Holland's solo debut, praised here as a credible, likable Spider-Man; the MJ reveal cited as proof storytelling can beat fan service.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Ryan Coogler's film, the first black filmmaker directing one of these franchises, with an all-black cast around a character elevated by Ta-Nehisi Coates's comics run.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: The third Avengers film and, the hosts guess, the start of Marvel handing the torch to its next generation of heroes.

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: An open question at the time: period setting unclear (the hosts guess the '80s or '90s), and how it plugs into the wider MCU still unknown.

films.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp
films.note: Floated, skeptically, as a potential pillar of the next phase.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: Referenced as inevitable ('there will be a Guardians 3'), with doubt about whether Chris Pratt's stardom can keep anchoring it.

Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
